memberdef.argsstring: (std::shared_ptr< BlockHeaderRepository > header_repo, std::shared_ptr< BlockStorage > storage, const primitives::BlockId &last_finalized_block, std::shared_ptr< network::ExtrinsicObserver > extrinsic_observer, std::shared_ptr< crypto::Hasher > hasher)
memberdef.name: create

detaileddescription.para:
Create an instance of block tree
header_repo
- block headers repository
storage
- block storage for the tree to be put in
last_finalized_block
- last finalized block, from which the tree is going to grow
extrinsic_observer
- extrinsic observer
hasher
- pointer to the hasher
ptr to the created instance or error

Get block header by provided block id
block
id of the block header we are looking for
result containing block header if it exists, error otherwise

Get a body (extrinsics) of the block (if present)
block
- id of the block to get body for
body, if the block exists in our storage, error in case it does not exist in our storage, or actual error happens

Get a justification of the block (if present)
block
- id of the block to get justification for
body, if the block exists in our storage, error in case it does not exist in our storage, or actual error happens

Adds header to the storage
header
that we are adding
result with success if header's parent exists on storage and new header was added. Error otherwise

Add a new block to the tree
block
to be added
nothing or error; if error happens, no changes in the tree are made
if block, which is specified in PARENT_HASH field of (block) is not in our local storage, corresponding error is returned. It is suggested that after getting that error, the caller would ask another peer for the parent block and try to insert it; this operation is to be repeated until a successful insertion happens

memberdef.argsstring: (primitives::BlockNumber block_number, const primitives::BlockHash &block_hash, const primitives::BlockBody &body) override
memberdef.name: addBlockBody

detaileddescription.para:
Adds block body to the storage
block_number
that corresponds to the block which body we are adding
block_hash
that corresponds to the block which body we are adding
block_body
that we are adding
result with success if block body was inserted. Error otherwise

Mark the block as finalized and store a finalization justification
block
to be finalized
justification
of the finalization
nothing or error

Get a chain of blocks from the specified block up to the closest finalized one
block
to get a chain from
chain of blocks in top-to-bottom order (from the last finalized block to the provided one) or error

memberdef.argsstring: (const primitives::BlockHash &block, bool ascending, uint64_t maximum) override
memberdef.name: getChainByBlock

detaileddescription.para:
Get a chain of blocks from the block
block
block from which the chain is started
ascending
- if true, the chain will grow up from the provided block (it is the lowest one); if false, down
maximum
number of blocks to be retrieved
chain or blocks or error

Get a chain of blocks
top_block
- block, which is at the top of the chain
bottom_block
- block, which is the bottom of the chain
chain of blocks in top-to-bottom order or error

Check if one block is ancestor of second one (direct chain exists)
ancestor
- block, which is at the top of the chain
descendant
- block, which is the bottom of the chain
true if ancestor is ancestor of descendant

Get a longest path (chain of blocks) from the last finalized block down to the deepest leaf
chain of blocks or error
this function is equivalent to "getChainByBlock(deepestLeaf())"

Get a deepest leaf of the tree
deepest leaf
deepest leaf is also a result of "SelectBestChain": if we are the leader, we connect a block, which we constructed, to that deepest leaf

memberdef.argsstring: (const primitives::BlockHash &target_hash, const boost::optional< primitives::BlockNumber > &max_number) const override
memberdef.name: getBestContaining

briefdescription.para: Get the most recent block of the best (longest) chain among those that contain a block with target_hash.

parameterdescription.para: is a hash of a block that the chosen chain must contain
is the max block number that the resulting block (and the target one) may possess

detaileddescription.para:
Get all leaves of our tree
collection of the leaves

Get children of the block with specified hash
block
to get children of
collection of children hashes or error

Get the last finalized block
hash of the block

memberdef.argsstring: (std::shared_ptr< BlockHeaderRepository > header_repo, std::shared_ptr< BlockStorage > storage, std::shared_ptr< TreeNode > tree, std::shared_ptr< TreeMeta > meta, std::shared_ptr< network::ExtrinsicObserver > extrinsic_observer, std::shared_ptr< crypto::Hasher > hasher)
memberdef.name: BlockTreeImpl

detaileddescription.para: Private constructor, so that instances are created only through the factory method

Walks the chain backwards starting from
start
until the current block number is less or equal than
limit

simplesect.para: the tree leaves sorted by their depth

memberdef.argsstring: (std::shared_ptr< TreeNode > node, std::vector< std::pair< primitives::BlockHash, primitives::BlockNumber > > &container)
memberdef.name: collectDescendants

detaileddescription.para: Block tree implementation
